commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: A maintainer/security review is needed to choose the non-forgeable task-runtime boundary; this is not a safe automated repair while the public SDK direction is still security-sensitive.; Needs attention: the diff introduces forgeable global task capability state that can bypass the intended host-issued scope boundary.; Review finding: [P1] Make task runtime scopes unforgeable — src/tasks/agent-harness-task-runtime-s... (sha=bbc4a50eb4445a737a675f73c6fc02b310c7ba44)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a Codex-native subagent monitor, transcript/task reconciliation, a generic agent-harness task-runtime SDK surface, and tests/docs/changelog for delivering child completions through parent sessions.

Reproducibility: yes. at source/proof level. Current main mirrors Codex-native child task lifecycle but lacks transcript completion reconciliation, while the PR body supplies live Discord steps and output showing the intended after-fix behavior.
commands.review_followup: A maintainer/security review is needed to choose the non-forgeable task-runtime boundary; this is not a safe automated repair while the public SDK direction is still security-sensitive.

target.title: fix(codex): deliver native spawn_agent completions
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Codex-native `spawn_agent` children can finish successfully, but OpenClaw may not mirror their lifecycle, wake the requester session, or deliver the synthesized parent reply visibly back to Discord.
- Why it matters: Mission Control shows stale running task rows and users have to manually poke the parent agent before the completed child result appears.
- What changed: add a Codex-native subagent monitor, a gateway-side transcript/task reconciler, and a completion delivery adapter that turns native child transcript completions into durable parent completion handoffs.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this does not replace the broader subagent delivery architecture or implement ACP/native `sessions_spawn` ownership policy; it handles Codex harness native `spawn_agent` child threads and their durable transcript completions.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #44925
- Related #52249
- Related #83352
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Codex-native `spawn_agent` worker completion now wakes the parent Scout session and produces a visible Discord reply without a manual follow-up ping.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw native install on macOS, gateway LaunchAgent, Discord Mission Control channel, Codex harness native `spawn_agent`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. Hot-patched the local installed OpenClaw gateway with the same source behavior in this PR.
  2. Restarted the gateway.
  3. From Discord `#mission-control`, asked the parent agent to spawn a worker to check weather using Codex native `spawn_agent`.
  4. Repeated after earlier failed attempts where the worker completed but the parent only replied after a manual poke.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```text
San Diego: 63°F and cloudy.

Notable forecast: Monday stays mostly cloudy with a high near 70°F, with SW wind gusts up to 20 mph.

Source: NOAA/NWS weather.gov, KSAN observation station.

End-to-end note: this reply is being sent visibly to <#1493670744029401119> from the subagent completion result.

Sent visibly to #mission-control.

Discord receipt: 1505787418349928589
```

Additional live test after packaging: a longer engineer analysis run completed and proactively returned the full worker response in Discord.

- Observed result after fix: the child worker completed, the parent Scout was re-entered without a user poke, and the final synthesized answer was sent visibly to the Discord Mission Control channel.
- What was not tested: Telegram/Slack/ACP paths, Docker install, and upstream CI before PR creation.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): repeated pre-fix live runs had completed worker results in the parent context, but the parent did not proactively reply until the user sent another Discord message.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Codex native `spawn_agent` owns the child thread lifecycle inside the Codex harness, so OpenClaw was not reliably converting child transcript terminal events into OpenClaw task completion and parent delivery state.
- Missing detection / guardrail: no durable gateway-side reconciler scanned Codex child transcripts for `event_msg.task_complete` / `task_failed` and retried parent completion delivery until the parent produced a visible response.
- Contributing context (if known): the old documented `sessions_spawn`/`sessions_yield` flow is not available in the Codex harness path; native `spawn_agent` produces usable child transcript state, but OpenClaw needs an adapter around it.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `extensions/codex/src/app-server/native-subagent-monitor.test.ts`
  - `extensions/codex/src/app-server/native-subagent-notification.test.ts`
  - `src/plugin-sdk/codex-native-task-runtime.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: native Codex child notifications and transcript completions create/mirror task rows, finalize terminal rows, mark delivery pending, wake the requester parent, and only mark delivered after a durable direct parent completion response.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it tests the OpenClaw/Codex adapter seams without needing private Discord credentials or a live Codex websocket.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing subagent announce tests covered generic completion delivery but did not cover Codex-native child transcript reconciliation.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Codex-native delegated workers that complete in the background should now proactively deliver their completion back through the parent session, including visible Discord channel replies, instead of waiting for the user to poke the parent agent.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
Discord parent -> Codex spawn_agent -> child transcript task_complete -> stale OpenClaw task / no proactive visible reply

After:
Discord parent -> Codex spawn_agent -> child transcript task_complete -> OpenClaw reconciler -> parent completion handoff -> visible Discord reply
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: native OpenClaw install, gateway LaunchAgent
- Model/provider: Codex harness, OpenAI Codex native `spawn_agent`
- Integration/channel (if any): Discord `#mission-control`
- Relevant config (redacted): Mission Control Discord channel session bound to Scout/parent agent

### Steps

1. From Discord `#mission-control`, ask the parent agent to spawn a worker using native `spawn_agent`.
2. Let the worker complete without sending a follow-up message.
3. Watch for a parent synthesized reply posted visibly in the same Discord channel.
4. Inspect task rows for `codex-thread:<child>` status and delivery status.

### Expected

- Worker completes.
- OpenClaw finalizes the mirrored `codex-native` task row.
- Parent Scout receives a completion handoff.
- Parent reply is sent visibly to Discord.
- Delivery is marked delivered only after the parent response path succeeds.

### Actual

- The live Discord weather test posted visibly to `#mission-control` with Discord receipt `1505787418349928589`.
- A later long engineer analysis run also completed and returned the full response proactively.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: Codex-native worker completion posts a visible Discord parent reply after gateway restart; longer engineer analysis run also completed and proactively returned.
- Edge cases checked: stale terminal rows are not blindly replayed; delivery remains pending when the parent handoff does not produce a durable direct completion response; Discord/channel completion uses the message-tool-only path when required.
- What you did **not** verify: ACP runtime, Docker install, non-Discord channels, and full upstream CI.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: duplicate parent replies if a live notification and transcript reconciliation both observe the same child completion.
  - Mitigation: task delivery state and completion dedupe keys prevent delivered rows from being reconciled again; the reconciler ignores already delivered/failed rows and old terminal rows outside the active/pending window.
- Risk: parent transcript completion exists but external channel delivery still fails.
  - Mitigation: Codex-native completions are treated as agent-mediated completion sources and require durable direct parent completion delivery before marking delivered.
target.author: bryanpearson

  3. From Discord `#mission-control`, asked the parent agent to spawn a worker to check weather using Codex native `spawn_agent`.
  4. Repeated after earlier failed attempts where the worker completed but the parent only replied after a manual poke.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```text
San Diego: 63°F and cloudy.

Notable forecast: Monday stays mostly cloudy with a high near 70°F, with SW wind gusts up to 20 mph.

Source: NOAA/NWS weather.gov, KSAN observation station.

End-to-end note: this reply is being sent visibly to <#1493670744029401119> from the subagent completion result.

Sent visibly to #mission-control.

Discord receipt: 1505787418349928589
```

Additional live test after packaging: a longer engineer analysis run completed and proactively returned the full worker response in Discord.

- Observed result after fix: the child worker completed, the parent Scout was re-entered without a user poke, and the final synthesized answer was sent visibly to the Discord Mission Control channel.
- What was not tested: Telegram/Slack/ACP paths, Docker install, and upstream CI before PR creation.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): repeated pre-fix live runs had completed worker results in the parent context, but the parent did not proactively reply until the user sent another Discord message.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Codex native `spawn_agent` owns the child thread lifecycle inside the Codex harness, so OpenClaw was not reliably converting child transcript terminal events into OpenClaw task completion and parent delivery state.
- Missing detection / guardrail: no durable gateway-side reconciler scanned Codex child transcripts for `event_msg.task_complete` / `task_failed` and retried parent completion delivery until the parent produced a visible response.
- Contributing context (if known): the old documented `sessions_spawn`/`sessions_yield` flow is not available in the Codex harness path; native `spawn_agent` produces usable child transcript state, but OpenClaw needs an adapter around it.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `extensions/codex/src/app-server/native-subagent-monitor.test.ts`
  - `extensions/codex/src/app-server/native-subagent-notification.test.ts`
  - `src/plugin-sdk/codex-native-task-runtime.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: native Codex child notifications and transcript completions create/mirror task rows, finalize terminal rows, mark delivery pending, wake the requester parent, and only mark delivered after a durable direct parent completion response.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it tests the OpenClaw/Codex adapter seams without needing private Discord credentials or a live Codex websocket.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing subagent announce tests covered generic completion delivery but did not cover Codex-native child transcript reconciliation.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Codex-native delegated workers that complete in the background should now proactively deliver their completion back through the parent session, including visible Discord channel replies, instead of waiting for the user to poke the parent agent.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
Discord parent -> Codex spawn_agent -> child transcript task_complete -> stale OpenClaw task / no proactive visible reply

After:
Discord parent -> Codex spawn_agent -> child transcript task_complete -> OpenClaw reconciler -> parent completion handoff -> visible Discord reply
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: native OpenClaw install, gateway LaunchAgent
- Model/provider: Codex harness, OpenAI Codex native `spawn_agent`
- Integration/channel (if any): Discord `#mission-control`
- Relevant config (redacted): Mission Control Discord channel session bound to Scout/parent agent

### Steps

1. From Discord `#mission-control`, ask the parent agent to spawn a worker using native `spawn_agent`.
2. Let the worker complete without sending a follow-up message.
3. Watch for a parent synthesized reply posted visibly in the same Discord channel.
4. Inspect task rows for `codex-thread:<child>` status and delivery status.

### Expected

- Worker completes.
- OpenClaw finalizes the mirrored `codex-native` task row.
- Parent Scout receives a completion handoff.
- Parent reply is sent visibly to Discord.
- Delivery is marked delivered only after the parent response path succeeds.

### Actual

- The live Discord weather test posted visibly to `#mission-control` with Discord receipt `1505787418349928589`.
- A later long engineer analysis run also completed and returned the full response proactively.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: Codex-native worker completion posts a visible Discord parent reply after gateway restart; longer engineer analysis run also completed and proactively returned.
- Edge cases checked: stale terminal rows are not blindly replayed; delivery remains pending when the parent handoff does not produce a durable direct completion response; Discord/channel completion uses the message-tool-only path when required.
- What you did **not** verify: ACP runtime, Docker install, non-Discord channels, and full upstream CI.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: duplicate parent replies if a live notification and transcript reconciliation both observe the same child completion.
  - Mitigation: task delivery state and completion dedupe keys prevent delivered rows from being reconciled again; the reconciler ignores already delivered/failed rows and old terminal rows outside the active/pending window.
- Risk: parent transcript completion exists but external channel delivery still fails.
  - Mitigation: Codex-native completions are treated as agent-mediated completion sources and require durable direct parent completion delivery before marking delivered.

target.title: fix(codex): deliver native spawn_agent completions
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Codex-native `spawn_agent` children can finish successfully, but OpenClaw may not mirror their lifecycle, wake the requester session, or deliver the synthesized parent reply visibly back to Discord.
- Why it matters: Mission Control shows stale running task rows and users have to manually poke the parent agent before the completed child result appears.
- What changed: add a Codex-native subagent monitor, a gateway-side transcript/task reconciler, and a completion delivery adapter that turns native child transcript completions into durable parent completion handoffs.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this does not replace the broader subagent delivery architecture or implement ACP/native `sessions_spawn` ownership policy; it handles Codex harness native `spawn_agent` child threads and their durable transcript completions.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #44925
- Related #52249
- Related #83352
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Codex-native `spawn_agent` worker completion now wakes the parent Scout session and produces a visible Discord reply without a manual follow-up ping.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw native install on macOS, gateway LaunchAgent, Discord Mission Control channel, Codex harness native `spawn_agent`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

  2. Restarted the gateway.
  3. From Discord `#mission-control`, asked the parent agent to spawn a worker to check weather using Codex native `spawn_agent`.
  4. Repeated after earlier failed attempts where the worker completed but the parent only replied after a manual poke.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

  - [ ] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient

  - `extensions/codex/src/app-server/native-subagent-notification.test.ts`
  - `src/plugin-sdk/codex-native-task-runtime.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: native Codex child notifications and transcript completions create/mirror task rows, finalize terminal rows, mark delivery pending, wake the requester parent, and only mark delivered after a durable direct parent completion response.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it tests the OpenClaw/Codex adapter seams without needing private Discord credentials or a live Codex websocket.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing subagent announce tests covered generic completion delivery but did not cover Codex-native child transcript reconciliation.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Codex-native delegated workers that complete in the background should now proactively deliver their completion back through the parent session, including visible Discord channel replies, instead of waiting for the user to poke the parent agent.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
Discord parent -> Codex spawn_agent -> child transcript task_complete -> stale OpenClaw task / no proactive visible reply

After:
Discord parent -> Codex spawn_agent -> child transcript task_complete -> OpenClaw reconciler -> parent completion handoff -> visible Discord reply
```
